english: one's turn / at it / involved / in a situation
notes: Colloquial short form of 'daran'. Used in phrases like 'du bist dran' (it's your turn), 'früh dran sein' (to be early), 'schlecht dran sein' (to be in a bad situation). Very common in spoken German.

sentences.english: It's your turn – roll the dice now!

sentences.german: Wir sind früh dran, der Zug fährt erst in einer Stunde.
sentences.english: We are early, the train doesn't leave for another hour.
